steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: P6 gave equal ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. But the model's predictions correlated differently with those ratings: 0.42 for abstract vs. 0.27 for cubist. The question asks what this difference in correlation implies about the model's predictions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘P6 would derive more aesthetic pleasure from abstract paintings than from cubist paintings.’: a higher correlation doesn't tell us the direction of the predictions, only their accuracy relative to P6's actual ratings. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘P6's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would equal one another.’: if predictions were equal for both, the correlations would be equally strong — contradicting the 0.42 vs. 0.27 gap. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘P6's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would differ from one another.’: the unequal correlations mean the model's predictions diverged between styles, so it must have assigned different predicted ratings to abstract vs. cubist. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘P6 would derive less aesthetic pleasure from abstract paintings than from cubist paintings.’: again, the correlation values don't determine direction — only that the model differentiated the two. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
